notes: Callers + walk-ins at ember.ash.rest.bbs. Some are dialed in (they live in the terminal); some came in through the alley door (they live in the couch corner). The BBS makes its own community across both.

visitors.name: The Regular Caller
visitors.lore_text: Dials in every night at 9. Sysop knows their handle, doesn't know their name. They tend to write the longer posts.

steps.greet: Their handle shows green on the terminal. They are typing.
steps.listen: They post a long thread. You read it as it comes in.
steps.deliver: You reply. You stamp the post into the keep-archive.
steps.sit_with: You sit at the CRT and answer them in the long form they came for.

visitors.name: The Walk-In
visitors.lore_text: Came through the alley door because they didn't want to type it. They wanted to say it.

steps.greet: They knock once. Then they let themselves in.
steps.listen: They sit on the couch and tell you what they would've posted.
steps.deliver: You bring them coffee with hot sauce because that is what is in the kitchen.
steps.sit_with: You sit on the couch with them. The modem rack hums.

visitors.name: The Lurker
visitors.lore_text: Has read every thread on the BBS for two years. Has never posted. Tonight they are going to.

steps.greet: Their handle shows up. It is a handle you have never seen post.
steps.listen: They post a single sentence. The sentence has weight.
steps.deliver: You reply gently. You do not reply at length. The lurker doesn't want length right now.
steps.sit_with: You sit at the CRT. They post a second sentence. It lands.

visitors.name: The Kid with Questions
visitors.lore_text: Sixteen. Has had a number of questions for sysop. Showed up to ask them in person because the BBS started feeling impersonal.

steps.greet: They are visibly trying not to seem nervous.
steps.listen: They ask. You answer. You answer in small pieces.
steps.deliver: You hand them a printed-out page from the archive. The page is from a thread two years ago that answered a similar question.
steps.sit_with: You sit with them at the printer table. They read the page. They thank you. They go home.

visitors.name: The Ham Radio Voice
visitors.lore_text: Came in on 14.301 MHz. A voice you only hear once a season. They want to talk for a while.

steps.listen: They tell you the weather where they are. The weather is the cover for the call.
steps.deliver: You give them the small archived signal-report they asked for last time.
steps.sit_with: You sit at the ham radio with them. They sign off slowly.

visitors.name: Anya · The Recording
visitors.lore_text: Anya — the recording — has come down for a floppy from the wall. She knows which one. She has known for a long time.

steps.greet: She doesn't speak. She gestures at the wall.
steps.listen: She points to a floppy. The floppy is labeled in your hand.
steps.deliver: You hand it to her. She holds it without inserting it.
steps.sit_with: You sit with her at the floppy wall. She still holds it. She doesn't put it down.

visitors.name: Sysop (off-shift)
visitors.lore_text: When the player is NOT Sysop, he comes down off-shift to make coffee. When the player IS Sysop, this swaps out.

steps.greet: He waves at you from the kitchen alcove.
steps.listen: He tells you what's been on the board today.
steps.deliver: You bring him a coffee. He thanks you.
steps.sit_with: You sit on the couch corner together. The room is itself.
